L1.area: Container instance lifecycle and configuration management

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists in the container credential provisioning mechanism. When writing credential files for guest system services, user-controlled credential names are used directly in file path construction without validation or confinement. An attacker with access to instance configuration can craft credential names containing directory traversal sequences to write arbitrary files on the host filesystem as root, enabling privilege escalation or denial of service.

L3.area: Container instance lifecycle and configuration management
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists in the container credential provisioning mechanism. When writing credential files for guest system services, user-controlled credential names are used directly in file path construction without validation or confinement. An attacker with access to instance configuration can craft credential names containing directory traversal sequences to write arbitrary files on the host filesystem as root, enabling privilege escalation or denial of service.

ground_truth.reason: The credential name from instance configuration is used to construct a file path via filepath.Join without sanitization, allowing directory traversal sequences in credential names to write arbitrary files on the host filesystem.
